records.title: NASA SBIR Award: Radiation Hardened, Programmable Battery Analog Front-End ASIC
records.authors_or_org: Alphacore, Inc. (Tempe, AZ) / NASA
records.claim_supported: NASA awarded Alphacore, Inc. $899,958 (period of performance July 27, 2024 - July 26, 2026) to develop a radiation-hardened, high-accuracy Battery Analog Front-End (B-AFE) ASIC for spacecraft power systems, incorporating Coulomb counting and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy on SOI process technology rated to exceed 300 krad(Si) -- a battery-management/state-estimation power-electronics sub-niche, complementing L09-106's battery hardware-demonstration record.
records.notes: Directly fetched sbir.gov award page this session; awardee, agency, dollar amount, award period, and abstract confirmed directly. Alphacore also holds a separate, distinct NASA SBIR award for radiation-tolerant high-voltage (100-1000Vdc) DC-DC converters for lunar/Mars missions per WebSearch context this session, but that award's specific sbir.gov page/ID and dollar figure could not be independently located/fetched this session (a 404 was returned for one candidate award ID) -- not included as a separate record to avoid an unconfirmed entry. | SCOUT: Directly fetched sbir.gov award page; awardee/agency/amount/period/abstract confirmed. VERIFIER: Independently re-fetched sbir.gov/awards/212066 this session -- confirms award title, awardee (Alphacore, Inc.), agency (NASA), total amount ($899,958), and period of performance (July 27, 2024 - July 26, 2026) exactly matching the scout's record. Official government primary award record. ACCEPTED T1 government, demand_evidence_type=official_project_award.

records.title: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Form 10-K -- Space Systems segment (SolAero solar power products, reaction wheels, spacecraft battery power systems)
records.authors_or_org: Rocket Lab USA, Inc.
records.claim_supported: Rocket Lab's FY2025 Form 10-K describes its Space Systems reportable segment (part of $601.8M FY2025 total revenue, vs. $436.2M FY2024 and $244.6M FY2023) as including vertically-integrated space solar cells/CICs/solar-array products (via the Jan 2022 SolAero Technologies Corp. acquisition) plus reaction-wheel flywheels and high-capacity, high-voltage spacecraft batteries -- an incumbent-map data point (vertically-integrated commercial supplier of spacecraft power hardware) not previously in this lane's ledger. NOTE: identity of the primary audited document could not be independently established this session (see notes); treat the specific dollar figures/segment wording as company-reported and not independently confirmed against the primary filing.
records.notes: IMPORTANT LIMITATION: direct WebFetch of the primary SEC.gov 10-K filing (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819994/000181999426000013/rklb-20251231.htm) returned HTTP 403 this session (SEC.gov blocks the fetch tool's request pattern); the quoted text above was obtained by fetching a third-party SEC-filing-aggregator page (stocktitan.net) that reproduces verbatim passages from the primary 10-K. This is disclosed transparently per claim-discipline rules; canonical_key points to the primary SEC filing for identity purposes, while the fetched/evidentiary url is the stocktitan page actually retrieved this session. Verifier should attempt an independent primary-source fetch (e.g., via an authenticated tool) before treating financial figures as fully confirmed. | SCOUT/ORCHESTRATOR FLAG: direct WebFetch of sec.gov 10-K returned HTTP 403; only stocktitan.net third-party aggregator was fetchable. VERIFIER TIER-DISCIPLINE DETERMINATION: Made an aggressive, multi-route re-fetch attempt this session specifically targeting SEC/primary-source identity: (1) sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar company-search -- HTTP 403; (2) data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK0001819994.json (SEC's own machine-readable filing-index API) -- HTTP 403; (3) direct sec.gov/Archives/.../rklb-20251231.htm (the canonical 10-K document itself) -- HTTP 403; (4) efts.sec.gov full-text-search API -- HTTP 403; (5) the underlying 8-K earnings-release exhibit at sec.gov/Archives/.../rklb-ex99_1.htm (located via WebSearch) -- HTTP 403; (6) Rocket Lab's own investor-relations domain investors.rocketlabusa.com -- redirected to investors.rocketlabcorp.com, which then timed out/connection-reset on two separate attempts; (7) Rocket Lab's own official earnings-release PDF at investors.rocketlabcorp.com/node/12026/pdf -- timed out. Every SEC.gov route and every Rocket-Lab-official route failed this session; only the stocktitan.net aggregator returned content, and it explicitly states it does NOT provide a direct hyperlink to the original SEC filing (confirmed via this session's own fetch of that page), which weakens (though does not eliminate) confidence in strict textual fidelity. Independent WebSearch this session found a consistent ~$602M FY2025 revenue figure and matching Space-Systems-segment/SolAero/reaction-wheel description via a separate aggregation path (investors.rocketlabcorp.com press release as surfaced through search), which corroborates the substance of the stocktitan figures but is still not a first-hand fetch of the audited 10-K itself. DECISION: because the audited primary document's identity/text could not be independently established via ANY route this session despite 7 distinct attempts, and per this wave's explicit tier-discipline mandate to resist waving records through as T1, this record is DOWNGRADED from the scout's proposed T1 to T2 (credible, cross-corroborated secondary reproduction of a company filing, not a confirmed-fidelity primary document). Retained as accepted (useful, plausible, non-vendor-marketing incumbent-map data point, internally consistent across two independent observation sessions) rather than rejected outright. Does not count toward the audited-company-filing T1 quota.

records.title: AIAA S-122 -- Electrical Power Systems for Unmanned Spacecraft
records.authors_or_org: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA); endorsed by NASA Office of the Chief Engineer
records.claim_supported: AIAA S-122 (published 2007; endorsed by NASA's Office of the Chief Engineer on November 3, 2017, as a non-mandatory reference standard) establishes design practices and verification/validation requirements for unmanned-spacecraft electrical power systems, primarily targeting earth-orbiting satellites using photovoltaic/battery power while remaining applicable to alternative power-generation/storage methods.
records.notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session. This is the US/AIAA-side counterpart to the European ECSS-E-ST-20-20C (L09-111) and ECSS-E-ST-20C Rev.2 (L09-044) power-system standards -- a useful US-vs-Europe standards-regime comparison point for this lane's 'US vs Asia/Europe differences' section, not previously represented (this ledger had only the European ECSS standards before this session). | SCOUT: Directly fetched and confirmed; US/AIAA counterpart to European ECSS standards. VERIFIER: Independently re-fetched standards.nasa.gov/standard/NASA/AIAA-S-122 this session -- confirms title, 2007 publication year, AIAA as issuing body with NASA Office of the Chief Engineer 2017 endorsement, and scope description (design practices/verification requirements for unmanned-spacecraft EPS, photovoltaic/battery focus but extensible to alternative power methods) matching the scout's record exactly. Official standards-body primary record; no peer-review applicable. ACCEPTED T1 standard.

records.claim_supported: Fortescue and XCMG signed an agreement under which XCMG is expected to supply up to half of Fortescue's future 300-400-truck fleet of zero-emission 240t haul trucks (XDE240 BEV), with phased deliveries planned 2028-2030, alongside a separate Liebherr T264 BEV order.
records.notes: Trade-press article fetched successfully this session; reports on a primary corporate announcement (Fortescue/XCMG) but the primary company press release itself was not independently fetched -> tiered T2 rather than T1 to be conservative. | Trade-press article fetched successfully by scout; reports on a primary corporate announcement (Fortescue/XCMG) but the primary company press release itself was not independently fetched -> tiered T2 rather than T1 to be conservative. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Fortescue fits out first 240 tonne electric haul truck, and rolls out first 6 MW fast charger
records.authors_or_org: Fortescue / Fortescue Zero (via thedriven.io reporting)
records.claim_supported: Fortescue commissioned its first in-house-developed 6 MW fast charger (approx. 30-minute full charge) for its 240-tonne, 2.6 MWh-battery Liebherr T264 Battery Electric haul truck, targeting operational status in the Pilbara before end of 2026 as part of a 300+ truck fleet plan.
records.notes: Trade-press article fetched successfully this session with direct quotes; primary Fortescue investor/media release not independently fetched -> tiered T2. | Trade-press article fetched successfully by scout with direct quotes; primary Fortescue investor/media release not independently fetched -> tiered T2. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Rio Tinto and BHP collaborate on battery-electric haul truck trials in the Pilbara
records.authors_or_org: Rio Tinto; BHP; Caterpillar; Komatsu
records.claim_supported: Announced 27 May 2024: BHP will trial two Caterpillar 793 battery-electric haul trucks (trials beginning second half of 2024) and Rio Tinto will trial two Komatsu 930 battery-electric haul trucks (trials beginning 2026), both in the Pilbara region of Western Australia, as a joint industry collaboration toward zero-emissions haulage.
records.notes: Primary company press release URL identified via WebSearch but not independently WebFetched this session (confirmed via search snippet synthesis and cross-referenced trade-press reporting: SME Mining Engineering Online, Electrek, Skillings). Tiered T1 as an official company/customer project announcement. | CORRECTION: primary press release fetched directly and successfully this session (raw record was WebSearch-synthesis only). The raw record's claim that 'two Cat 793 XE trucks' were trialed at BHP's Jimblebar mine by both companies is INCORRECT -- the actual fetched page states BHP tests Caterpillar 793 trucks while Rio Tinto separately tests Komatsu 930 trucks (4 trucks total, 2 manufacturers, 2 companies). The raw record's 'Jimblebar' mine name and '100+ operating hours / 200+ test laps by late 2025' figures were NOT present in the content fetched this session and are not re-confirmed -- removed from claim_supported rather than carried forward unverified.

records.title: Rio Tinto and China's State Power Investment Corporation launch battery swap truck trial fleet at Oyu Tolgoi mine
records.authors_or_org: Rio Tinto; State Power Investment Corporation (SPIC) Qiyuan
records.claim_supported: Rio Tinto and China's SPIC Qiyuan deployed 8 battery-swap-electric Tonly haul trucks (91-tonne) with 13 x 800kWh batteries, a swap station, and static charger at the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine (Mongolia), announced 27 October 2025, with swap times under 7 minutes and testing running through end of 2026.
records.notes: Primary company press release fetched successfully this session; all figures (fleet size, battery count/capacity, swap time) confirmed directly from the page. Geography includes Mongolia (mine location) and China (SPIC, technology/equipment origin). | Primary company press release fetched successfully by scout; all figures confirmed directly from the page. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Union Pacific Railroad Invests in Wabtec's FLXdrive Battery-Electric Locomotive
records.authors_or_org: Union Pacific Railroad
records.claim_supported: Union Pacific ordered 10 Wabtec FLXdrive battery-electric locomotives (~2.5 MWh each, ~7,000 cells) on 28 January 2022, described as the largest North American railroad investment in battery technology, targeting zero-emission yard operations and projected to eliminate 4,000 tons of carbon annually.
records.notes: Primary company press release fetched successfully this session; figures confirmed directly. | Primary company press release fetched successfully by scout; figures confirmed directly. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Vale Partners with Wabtec on Alternative Fuels Study and Orders Three FLXdrive Battery-Electric Locomotives
records.authors_or_org: Wabtec Corporation; Vale S.A.
records.claim_supported: Vale ordered 3 Wabtec FLXdrive battery-electric locomotives (13 July 2023) to replace diesel 'dynamic helper' units on the Carajas Railroad (EFC), the world's largest iron-ore train (330 railcars, 45,000 tons); Wabtec estimates ~25 million litres of diesel saved and ~63,000 tons CO2 avoided annually; delivery expected 2026 from Wabtec's Contagem, Brazil plant.
records.notes: Primary company press release fetched successfully this session; figures confirmed directly. | Primary company press release fetched successfully by scout; figures confirmed directly. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: 内蒙古太平2026-2028年露天采剥铲装运输设备租赁 (Inner Mongolia Taiping 2026-2028 Open-Pit Mining Stripping/Loading/Transport Equipment Leasing Tender)
records.authors_or_org: 中国黄金集团建设有限公司内蒙古乌拉特中旗分公司 (China Gold Group Construction Co., Ltd., Inner Mongolia Urad Middle Banner Branch)
records.claim_supported: China Gold Group's Inner Mongolia branch issued an open-pit mining equipment leasing tender (2026-2028, mining volume 5.31M m3, requiring a minimum fleet of 60 dump trucks [40x70t + 15x60t] plus 9 excavators); registration deadline 2 Feb 2026, bids due 27 Feb 2026, contract through end-2028 -- a live conventional-equipment tender that establishes baseline fleet-size/spec demand against which electrification alternatives can be benchmarked.
records.notes: Fetched directly from the official state-owned-enterprise e-tender portal this session; content in Chinese. Note this specific tender is for conventional (not explicitly battery-electric) equipment leasing -- included as a primary demand/fleet-scale baseline record for China's open-pit mining equipment market, not as direct electrification-specific demand evidence. | Fetched directly from the official state-owned-enterprise e-tender portal by scout; content in Chinese. This is a conventional (not explicitly battery-electric) equipment leasing tender -- included as a primary demand/fleet-scale baseline record, not direct electrification-specific demand evidence. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: 巴润分公司露天矿5G+工程机械远程智能化控制项目 (Barun Branch Open-Pit Mine 5G + Construction Machinery Remote Intelligent Control Project)
records.authors_or_org: 内蒙古包钢钢联股份有限公司 (Inner Mongolia Baogang Steel Union Co., Ltd., Barun Mining Branch)
records.claim_supported: Baogang Steel Union's open-pit Barun mine tendered a phase-two 5G + remote-intelligent-control retrofit for 4 dozers and 1 roller (procurement estimate ~RMB3.75M of ~RMB18.06M total project investment); tender documents available 3-8 July 2026, bids due 29 July 2026 09:30 -- evidence of active remote/autonomous-control retrofit investment in Chinese open-pit mining construction machinery (an adjacent enabling technology to electrification).
records.notes: Fetched directly from the official Inner Mongolia public-tender platform this session; content in Chinese. Tender is for remote-control retrofit, not battery-electric conversion specifically -- included as adjacent-technology demand evidence for the same buyer/site category as battery-electric mining equipment. | Fetched directly from the official Inner Mongolia public-tender platform by scout; content in Chinese. Tender is for remote-control retrofit, not battery-electric conversion specifically. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Ports as Power Plants: Inside the Maersk-CATL Alliance for Maritime Decarbonization
records.authors_or_org: Maersk; CATL (via CleanTechnica reporting)
records.claim_supported: Maersk and CATL signed an MOU (10 October 2025) to collaborate on decarbonizing logistics, port operations, and supply chains, targeting electrification of yard tractors, reach stackers, and cranes with battery support/recycling, building on a June 2025 CATL-APM Terminals agreement to electrify port equipment and deploy large battery systems (potentially serving as port power buffers/grid services).
records.notes: Trade-press article fetched successfully this session; reports on a primary corporate MOU but the primary Maersk/CATL press releases were not independently fetched -> tiered T2. | Trade-press article fetched successfully by scout; reports on a primary corporate MOU but the primary Maersk/CATL press releases were not independently fetched -> tiered T2. records.title: Norway Orders World's First Battery-Power Autonomous Ferries (Fjord1 / Tersan Shipyard, Sognefjorden route)
records.authors_or_org: Fjord1; Norwegian Public Roads Administration; Tersan Shipyard (via Maritime Executive reporting)
records.claim_supported: Fjord1, under a Norwegian Public Roads Administration development contract, ordered 4 fully battery-electric double-ended RoRo ferries (120 vehicles each) from Turkey's Tersan Shipyard for the Lavik-Oppedal (Sognefjorden) route, announced 28 February 2024, with service entry planned Sept 2026 and full autonomous navigation by 2028.
records.notes: Trade-press article fetched successfully this session; reports a government-contracted procurement but the primary Norwegian Public Roads Administration/Fjord1 contract documentation was not independently fetched -> tiered T2. | Trade-press article fetched successfully by scout; reports a government-contracted procurement but the primary Norwegian Public Roads Administration/Fjord1 contract documentation was not independently fetched -> tiered T2. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: Jining '6006' Pure Electric Cargo Ship Selected as a National Benchmark
records.authors_or_org: CATL (Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited)
records.claim_supported: The Jining '6006' pure-electric multi-purpose cargo ship (67.6m, ~2,000t capacity, 2 x containerized power sources totaling 3,919 kWh, 230km range, 15-min battery swap) operates on the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal and was named a 'Landmark Case of National Transportation and Energy Integration Innovation Development in 2025' by the China Communications and Transportation Association -- China's first battery-swap cargo-ship demonstration with independent ship-station delivery, saving 50.4 tons of fuel annually.
records.notes: Primary company (vendor) press release fetched successfully this session; fetched page was the English-language version of CATL's site (language recorded as 'en', not 'zh', reflecting the specific page fetched) even though the underlying vessel/geography/benchmark designation is Chinese. | Primary company (vendor) press release fetched successfully by scout. RECLASSIFIED demand_evidence_type from 'direct_customer_documentation' to 'official_project_award' this session -- the load-bearing 'official' hook is the third-party national-benchmark designation by the China Communications and Transportation Association, not vendor-customer documentation per se.

records.title: YILPORT Places Order for 53 E-Hybrid RTG Cranes from Konecranes
records.authors_or_org: YILPORT Holding; Konecranes (via Container News reporting)
records.claim_supported: YILPORT Holding ordered 53 Konecranes E-Hybrid RTG cranes (20 automated + 33 manual) for terminals in Portugal (Liscont, Leixoes), El Salvador (Acajutla), and Ghana (Takoradi), recorded Q2 2026, with delivery in batches over the following two years -- a multi-country port-electrification procurement signal from a single terminal operator group.
records.notes: Trade-press article fetched successfully this session with full order breakdown by terminal; primary Konecranes/YILPORT press release not independently fetched -> tiered T2. YILPORT is Turkish-headquartered (Yildirim Group). | Trade-press article fetched successfully by scout with full order breakdown by terminal; primary Konecranes/YILPORT press release not independently fetched -> tiered T2. records.title: IMO approves net-zero regulations for global shipping; adoption session subsequently adjourned to October 2026
records.authors_or_org: International Maritime Organization (IMO)
records.claim_supported: IMO's MEPC 83 (7-11 April 2025) approved the Net-Zero Framework: mandatory well-to-wake GHG fuel-intensity limits plus a global pricing/reward mechanism for ships over 5,000 GT (85% of international shipping CO2). A subsequent extraordinary MEPC session (14-17 October 2025), convened to formally adopt the framework, was instead adjourned by member-state vote; IMO's own press page confirms the session 'has agreed to adjourn' and 'will be reconvened in 12 months' time' (i.e., October 2026), pushing possible entry into force to 2027-2028 at the earliest.
records.notes: Official IMO page fetched successfully this session. Note: as of this session, formal adoption remained adjourned to October 2026 per subsequent WebSearch results (zerocarbonshipping.com, DNV) -- the IMO page itself frames approval as of April 2025; the adjournment/entry-into-force slippage to 'at the earliest 1 March 2028' is cross-referenced from a separate source and should be reconciled by the verifier. | RESOLVED: the raw record's flagged tension between the April 2025 'approval' framing and the October 2025 adjournment (previously only cross-referenced from non-IMO sources) is now confirmed directly from a second official IMO press page fetched this session, removing the prior reconciliation caveat.

records.title: Renewable and low-carbon fuels in maritime transport (FuelEU Maritime Regulation (EU) 2023/1805)
records.authors_or_org: European Union (European Parliament and Council)
records.claim_supported: Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 'FuelEU Maritime' (adopted 13 Sept 2023, applied from 1 Jan 2025) requires passenger and container ships to use onshore power supply (OPS) or a zero-emission alternative for all electricity needs while moored at major EU (TEN-T core) ports from 1 January 2030 (extending to all ports from 2035), with penalty revenue required to fund maritime decarbonization projects, alongside a GHG-intensity reduction trajectory (2% in 2025 rising to 80% by 2050).
records.notes: Official EUR-Lex summary fetched successfully this session; specific penalty rate (EUR1.50/unused kWh, per cross-referenced trade sources) not independently confirmed on this specific fetched page -- flagged for verifier to confirm against the full regulation text or a class-society summary (e.g., DNV, LR). | Official EUR-Lex summary fetched successfully by scout. Specific penalty rate (EUR1.50/unused kWh, per cross-referenced trade sources) still not independently confirmed on this fetched page -- flagged for idea-architect stage to confirm against full regulation text if load-bearing. Not independently re-verified this session.

records.title: CARB to repeal Advanced Clean Fleets' High Priority Fleet and Drayage Fleet zero-emission provisions
records.authors_or_org: California Renewable Transportation Alliance (ca-rta.org), reporting on California Air Resources Board (CARB) action
records.claim_supported: CARB's Advanced Clean Fleets regulation required newly registering drayage trucks at California seaports/intermodal railyards to be zero-emission from 1 Jan 2024, with a full zero-emission drayage fleet target by 2035. Following a legal settlement with a 17-state coalition (led by Nebraska) and trucking-industry groups, CARB voted (25 Sept 2025) to repeal the High-Priority Fleet and Drayage Fleet provisions, with repeal expected to take effect 1 January 2027 (formal action required by 31 August 2026); CARB separately withdrew its EPA waiver request for the ACF's 2036 100%-ZEV-sales mandate in January 2025 and cannot enforce that mandate without a new waiver -- a live, confirmed regulatory reversal directly affecting port/drayage equipment electrification demand.
records.notes: RECLASSIFIED: the primary regulator page (ww2.arb.ca.gov) returned HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed on every fetch attempt across both the scout and this verifier session (2 separate attempts, same and different CARB URLs) -- fetch routes on the official CARB domain are exhausted. Instead, California Renewable Transportation Alliance (a recognized industry trade association per SOURCE_STANDARDS' T2 definition) was fetched successfully this session, providing more precise and more current detail (Sept 25 2025 vote; Jan 1 2027 effective repeal; Jan 2025 EPA waiver withdrawal) than the raw record. Source_type changed from 'regulator' to 'market_industry' and tier confirmed T2 to honestly reflect that the CARB page itself was never independently fetched, only trade-association reporting on the CARB action.

records.title: 船舶应用电池动力规范 2025年版 (Rules for Battery-Powered Ships, 2025 edition)
records.authors_or_org: 中国船级社 China Classification Society (CCS)
records.claim_supported: China Classification Society's 2025-edition 'Rules for Battery-Powered Ships' (effective 31 Dec 2025, superseding the 2023 version and 2024 amendments) governs design/construction/inspection of vessels using rechargeable batteries as part or all of propulsion power, adding digital inspection verification, shore-based monitoring platforms, containerized mobile power source inspection, automated system specs, and online monitoring data-exchange protocols.
records.notes: Official classification-society (standards-body) page fetched successfully this session in Chinese; effective date and scope confirmed directly. | Official classification-society (standards-body) page fetched successfully by scout in Chinese. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: 土方机械 纯电动液压挖掘机换电系统 通用要求 (GB/T 45926-2025: Earth-moving machinery -- Battery swap system of electric hydraulic excavators -- General requirements)
records.authors_or_org: 国家标准化管理委员会 Standardization Administration of China (SAC), via openstd.samr.gov.cn
records.claim_supported: China's national standard GB/T 45926-2025, 'Earth-moving machinery -- Battery swap system of electric hydraulic excavators -- General requirements,' was published 30 June 2025 and takes effect 1 January 2026, specifying general requirements for battery-swap systems on pure-electric hydraulic excavators -- confirming standards-body-level formalization of battery-swap infrastructure for electric construction/mining equipment in China.
records.notes: MAJOR UPDATE: the raw record's original URL (kass.sacinfo.org.cn) failed with an SSL/protocol error in both the scout and this verifier session (2 independent attempts), and the formal standard number was unconfirmed. This session, a WebSearch located and a WebFetch successfully confirmed the correct official record on openstd.samr.gov.cn (China's national standards public-service platform), resolving the exact standard number (GB/T 45926-2025), publication date (30 June 2025), and effective date (1 January 2026) directly from an authoritative government source. URL and canonical_key updated accordingly.

records.authors_or_org: US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
records.claim_supported: EPA's Clean Ports Program, funded with $3 billion under the Inflation Reduction Act, awarded 53 grants totaling nearly $3 billion (notified Oct 2024, finalized Dec 2024-Jan 2025) across 26 states/territories to fund zero-emission port equipment, infrastructure, and climate/air-quality planning, with 3-4 year implementation windows.

The specific Port of Oakland $322m figure cited in earlier WebSearch synthesis was NOT independently confirmed on this fetched page and remains excluded from claim_supported. records.title: Megawatt Charging System (MCS) technology page and IEC TS 63379 standardization
records.authors_or_org: CharIN e.V. (Charging Interface Initiative); International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
records.claim_supported: The Megawatt Charging System (MCS) standard supports up to 1,250V/3,000A DC (over 3.75 MW peak), with CharIN-member contributions formalized in IEC TS 63379 (published Feb 2026) for global interoperability; targeted at Class 6-8 trucks, buses, ferries/marine vessels, light electric aircraft, and heavy-duty mining equipment.
records.notes: Official CharIN consortium page fetched successfully this session. Reclassified from 'market_industry' to 'standard' / T1 because IEC TS 63379 is a formally published IEC technical specification (not merely vendor marketing), consistent with SOURCE_STANDARDS' T1 definition covering official standards. Geography (DE) reflects CharIN e.V.'s German registered-association headquarters, not a claim about IEC's or the standard's true multinational origin. | Official CharIN consortium page fetched successfully by scout; formally published IEC technical specification, not vendor marketing. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

records.title: FLXdrive Battery-Electric Locomotive (product page)
records.authors_or_org: Wabtec Corporation
records.claim_supported: Wabtec's FLXdrive product line spans Heavy-Haul (7.0 MWh battery / 3.2 MW traction), Switcher (2.7 MWh / 1.7 MW), Shunter (1.2 MWh / 0.7 MW), and a European Dual-Mode variant (1.5-2.0 MWh / 0.8-5.5 MW), using liquid-cooled batteries with a proprietary thermal-management system, regenerative braking, and DC fast-charging via stationary pantograph; marketed as the world's first 100% battery-electric heavy-haul locomotive. No public pricing is disclosed.
records.notes: Vendor product page fetched successfully this session; no price data was available on the page (a recurring pattern for heavy-electrification hardware, consistent with the L02/L08 lanes' prior findings of opaque vendor pricing). | Vendor product page fetched successfully by scout; no price data available (recurring opacity pattern across this lane). records.authors_or_org: Liebherr Mining Equipment
records.claim_supported: Liebherr's T264 Battery Electric mining truck offers 240t payload, static charging in under an hour, compatibility with a Trolley Assist overhead-pantograph system and 'Liebherr Power Rail' side-mounted dynamic-charging technology, and a claimed 86% reduction in energy cost per tonne versus diesel for a full electric fleet. records.title: R1700 XE Underground Battery-Electric LHD Loader (product specifications)
records.authors_or_org: Caterpillar Inc.
records.claim_supported: Caterpillar's R1700 XE is a zero-tailpipe-emission battery-electric underground LHD loader: 715V nominal lithium-ion battery pack, 213 kWh total energy capacity, switched-reluctance traction motor (335 hp peak / 295 hp nominal), charges in under 20 minutes with two Cat MEC500 chargers (840 kW combined) or under 30 minutes with one (500 kW), liquid (chilled coolant) battery cooling, rated operating range -20C to 50C. No public price is disclosed.

records.title: Hitachi Construction Machinery / ABB full-battery mining dump truck (EH4000) reaches commissioning at Kansanshi mine, Zambia
records.authors_or_org: Hitachi Construction Machinery; ABB; First Quantum Minerals (via International Mining trade-press reporting)
records.claim_supported: ABB and Hitachi Construction Machinery jointly developed (since June 2021) the Hitachi EH4000 'Full Battery Dump Truck,' the world's first ultra-large fully electric haul truck, using a hybrid battery + trolley-assist (overhead power line) + regenerative-braking system. A technological feasibility trial ran at First Quantum Minerals' Kansanshi copper-gold mine (Zambia) from June 2024 to August 2025, during which the truck covered over 4,000 km and transported more than 30,000 tonnes; a follow-on 'Battery Truck & Electrification Showcase' technical demonstration for mining customers was held at Kansanshi on 15-16 April 2026, with commercial deployment targeted for fiscal year 2027.
records.notes: RECLASSIFIED and UPDATED: the primary ABB press release (new.abb.com) failed to fetch (timeout, then socket error) across two attempts in two sessions; a direct Hitachi Construction Machinery press-release page (hitachicm.com) also returned HTTP 403 this session. Instead, International Mining trade-press reporting was fetched successfully this session, providing more current and more precise detail (EH4000 model name, exact trial distance/tonnage, FY2027 commercial-deployment target) than the raw record's ABB-sourced claim. Source_type changed from 'market_industry' to 'trade_press' to honestly reflect the actually-fetched source.

records.title: Singapore Introduces New Standard for Electric Harbour Craft (e-HC) Charging Infrastructure (Technical Reference TR 136)
records.authors_or_org: Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA); Enterprise Singapore
records.claim_supported: MPA and Enterprise Singapore published Technical Reference (TR) 136 (25 March 2025) establishing safety requirements for charging infrastructure and battery-swap systems for electric harbour craft (e-HC), protecting personnel/property from electrical and mechanical hazards and promoting interoperability (aligned with CCS, IEC 61851, IEC 62840); from 2030 all new harbour craft operating in the Port of Singapore must be fully electric, B100-biofuel-capable, or compatible with net-zero fuels.
records.notes: Official MPA (Singapore government statutory board) page fetched successfully this session; TR136 scope, date, and the 2030 all-electric-harbour-craft requirement confirmed directly from the fetched page. | Official MPA (Singapore government statutory board) page fetched successfully by scout. records.title: CO electrolysis to multicarbon products over grain boundary-rich Cu nanoparticles in membrane electrode assembly electrolyzers
records.authors_or_org: Li, H.; Wei, P.; Liu, T.; Li, M.; Wang, C.; Li, R.; Ye, J.; Zhou, Z.-Y.; Sun, S.-G.; Fu, Q.; Gao, D.; Wang, G.; Bao, X.
records.claim_supported: Nature Communications (vol. 15, 2024-05-30) primary research: grain boundary-rich Cu nanoparticle catalyst in an alkaline MEA electrolyzer achieved C2+ partial current density up to 4.35 +/- 0.07 A/cm^2 at 2.78 +/- 0.01 V cell voltage with 87 +/- 1% C2+ Faradaic efficiency.

records.title: Carbon dioxide electroreduction to C2 products over copper-cuprous oxide derived from electrosynthesized copper complex

records.claim_supported: Nature Communications (vol. 10, 2019-08-26) primary research: a Cu2O/Cu catalyst derived from an electrosynthesized copper complex drives CO2 electroreduction toward C2 products, an early foundational result in the Cu multicarbon-CO2RR catalyst design lineage that L11-005/006/007 build on.
records.notes: Replaces a review-tier placeholder (Nature Reviews Materials copper-stability piece) with a primary research paper to raise the T1 share of this lane's set; the review angle on copper instability is still available via L11-024's seawater-adjacent synthesis discussion and should be re-added by the verifier if a dedicated copper-stability review is needed. | Full author list now confirmed via Crossref.
